---

## What this business is

Kimbell Royalty Partners, LP is a Fort Worth-based publicly traded partnership that owns mineral and overriding royalty interests across U.S. oil and gas basins and pays monthly cash distributions to common unitholders. **[Fact]** The partnership elects to be taxed as a corporation for U.S. federal income tax purposes (`10-K_20260226`, Overview). **[Fact]** As an owner of non-cost-bearing interests, KRP receives a share of production revenues net of post-production costs and taxes but does not fund drilling, lease operating expense, or plugging and abandonment (`same 10-K`). **[Fact]** As of February 20, 2026, **93,396,488** common units were outstanding (`same 10-K`). **[Fact]** FY2025 total revenues were **$333.8 million**, operating income **$132.8 million**, and net income per common unit **$0.62** (`same 10-K`, consolidated statements). **[Fact]** FY2025 cash distributions paid to common unitholders totaled **$149.4 million**, or about **$1.60** per unit (`same 10-K`, cash flow statement).

---

## Why the market might be wrong

Yield-oriented investors may treat KRP as a simple bond proxy and miss the embedded acreage option on **~12.3 million gross mineral acres** and **~4.7 million gross ORRI acres**, with more than half of aggregate acreage in the Permian and Mid-Continent. **[Inference]** Screeners that rank on trailing GAAP earnings rather than cash distributions understate owner yield because depreciation and acquisition accounting suppress net income per unit relative to cash paid. **[Inference]** The partnership's **$441.5 million** revolver balance and Apollo-affiliated preferred equity may be priced as permanent leverage overhang rather than as financing for accretive acquisitions that expand the royalty footprint. **[Inference]** Q1 2026 revenue and earnings softness may be extrapolated as structural when FY2025 production still rose to **25,760 Boe/d** (`10-K_20260226`).

---

## Executive summary

Kimbell Royalty Partners is a passive U.S. mineral and royalty owner that distributes essentially all available cash monthly while funding growth through acquisitions and occasional equity issuance. FY2025 revenues reached **$333.8 million** and distributions about **$1.60** per unit on **93.4 million** units; proved reserves were **72,944 MBoe** (51% liquids) with average production **25,760 Boe/d**. The partnership carries **$441.5 million** on its **$625 million** secured revolver at year-end 2025, which caps dhando versus debt-free royalty trusts. At a placeholder **$13.50** per unit and **$1.60** starting distributions, Marvin's Lawrence base case annual return is filled by mechanical refresh; stance is **watch** pending live price and a return above our **~15%** bar. No approved third-party sources are in the base IRR. Undeveloped net acres remain an overlay option, not in Lawrence owner cash.

## Business & moat

### What (Stahl + Lawrence bucket)

| Field | Value | Evidence |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Archetype | optionality | Mineral/ORRI asset base + producing cash + undeveloped acreage |
| Cycle | mid | FY2025 revenues and production up; Q1 2026 YoY down |
| Lawrence bucket | other | Commodity-linked passive royalty, not operating moat |
| Payoff lens | asset | Distribution yield plus undeveloped acreage option |

### Mental models in plain English

**Permutations & combinations (Stahl):** Owner cash can rise from commodity price, production on existing royalties, or new mineral/ORRI acquisitions. **[Fact]** FY2025 production was **9.40 MMBoe** (**25,760 Boe/d**), up from **9.10 MMBoe** in 2024 (`10-K_20260226`).

**Low risk, high uncertainty (Munger):** KRP does not operate wells; downside is commodity and acquisition discipline, not operating blow-ups. **[Fact]** The partnership is not obligated to fund capex on third-party operations (`10-K`, Overview).

**Market structure discount (Horizon Kinetics):** Mineral royalty LPs are often excluded from simple dividend screens despite high cash payout ratios. **[Inference]** Taxable-as-corporation structure avoids K-1 complexity but also removes some MLP yield-buyer bid.

**Inversion (Munger):** Thesis fails if distributions fall sustainably below **$1.20** per unit while unit count rises from dilutive equity deals and revolver balance stays elevated.

### Business mechanics (Hohn)

#### Operating snapshot

| Metric | Latest | Prior | Source |
|--------|--------|-------|--------|
| FY2025 total revenues | **$333.8M** | $309.3M (2024) | 10-K FY2025 |
| FY2025 operating income | **$132.8M** | $37.0M (2024) | 10-K FY2025 |
| FY2025 net income per common unit | **$0.62** | $0.12 (2024) | 10-K FY2025 |
| FY2025 cash distributions paid (common) | **$149.4M** (~**$1.60**/unit) | $135.9M (2024) | 10-K cash flow |
| Common units outstanding (Feb 2026) | **93.4M** | n/a | 10-K cover |
| Proved reserves (Dec 31, 2025) | **72,944 MBoe** (51% liquids) | n/a | 10-K / Ryder Scott |
| Average daily production FY2025 | **25,760 Boe/d** | 24,868 Boe/d (2024) | 10-K |
| Revolver borrowings (Dec 31, 2025) | **$441.5M** | n/a | 10-K debt footnote |
| Revolver borrowing base | **$625M** | n/a | 10-K |
| Q1 2026 total revenues | **$65.5M** | $84.2M (Q1 2025) | 10-Q Mar 31, 2026 |
| Q1 2026 net income per unit | **$0.04** | $0.20 (Q1 2025) | 10-Q |

**Run-rate note:** Monthly distribution declarations lag production; use trailing twelve-month cash distributions paid, not a single quarter annualized.

#### Segment map

KRP reports **one operating segment** (royalty and mineral interests). **[Fact]** `NumberOfOperatingSegments: 1` (`10-K_20260226`, segment footnote). For overlay purposes we split **producing royalties** (in Lawrence cash flow) from **undeveloped acreage** (option row below).

| Segment | Type | Base treatment |
|---------|------|----------------|
| Producing mineral + ORRI cash | operating | Owner distributions; Lawrence consolidated path |
| Undeveloped mineral / ORRI acreage | option | **probability_weighted** in overlay; **$0** in Lawrence base |

#### Option scan

| # | Question | Answer | Treatment | Evidence |
|---|----------|--------|-----------|----------|
| 1 | GAAP book misstates core assets? | Partial | embedded_in_segment | Interests on balance sheet; reserve PV can diverge from carrying value |
| 2 | Undeveloped reserves / acreage? | Yes | probability_weighted | ~127,832 gross (9,760 net) undeveloped mineral; ~18,554 gross (92 net) undeveloped ORRI (`10-K` asset tables) |
| 5 | Private stakes below fair value? | No | n/a | Public partnership |
| 6 | Transitory distribution / legal recovery? | No | n/a | Q1 2026 weakness is activity/commodity, not trust-style suspension |
| 7 | Embedded option in revenue? | Yes | embedded_in_segment | Producing royalties; growth via acquisitions in MD&A |

#### Thesis pillars

| # | Pillar | Mechanism | Evidence |
|---|--------|-----------|----------|
| 1 | Monthly distribution yield | Partnership objective is increasing cash distributions (`10-K`, Overview) | Business section |
| 2 | Commodity linkage | Revenue tied to oil, gas, NGL production on underlying acreage | 10-K risk factors |
| 3 | Acreage scale | ~12.3M gross mineral + ~4.7M gross ORRI acres; 54% Permian/Mid-Continent | 10-K asset overview |
| 4 | Acquisition engine | Bolt-on deals funded with equity and revolver (`10-K` MD&A, 8-K deal flow) | 10-K / 8-K |
| 5 | Leverage constraint | **$441.5M** revolver reduces cash available for distribution | 10-K debt footnote |

#### Moat (Munger)

**Narrow, not widening:** Royalties are contractual and passive but fully commoditized; there is no pricing power on hydrocarbons. Durability comes from diversified acreage footprint, low corporate overhead, and acquisition access, not switching costs. **[Inference]**

#### Catalyst path

- **Base catalyst** (multi-year): Mid-cycle distributions with bolt-on mineral/ORRI deals offsetting decline
- **Bull catalyst:** Permian rig recovery converts undeveloped net acres; accretive deals raise per-unit cash
- **Bear catalyst:** Commodity down-cycle plus revolver amortization cuts distributions

## Payoff & return

### Five-question gate

| Gate | Pass? | Note |
|------|-------|------|
| 1 Understand? | Yes | Passive royalty LP; no operating capex |
| 2 Durable cash? | Partial | Depletion offset by acquisitions; commodity cyclical |
| 3 Bounded bear? | Partial | Distribution cuts and dilution; **$441.5M** revolver is real |
| 4 Cheap vs normalized? | See valuation | ~**$1.60**/unit FY2025 distributions vs price |
| 5 Why mispriced? | Partial | Yield vs GAAP earnings; acreage option; leverage overhang |
| 6 Aligned management? | [HUMAN REVIEW] | Sponsor/founder ownership; Apollo preferred; acquisition approval |

### Dhando (Pabrai)

**Partial:** Downside is commodity-driven distribution cuts and revolver service, not operating bankruptcy, but leverage and preferred equity reduce the "tails I don't lose much" cushion versus debt-free trusts like Dorchester Minerals.

### Stance proposal

Lawrence base annual return and normalized yield are computed in **## Valuation & IRR** after mechanical refresh. Placeholder stance: **watch** (distribution yield attractive; IRR likely below **~15%** bar at current price; debt caps conviction).

**Scenarios and every IRR assumption:** see **## Valuation & IRR (assumption ledger)** and `valuation.json`.

### Optionality overlay

| Lens | Reading | Source |
|------|---------|--------|
| Normalized distribution yield | ~**11.9%** on **$1.60**/unit at placeholder price | FY2025 distributions / unit |
| Undeveloped acreage option | **9,760 net** undeveloped mineral + **92 net** undeveloped ORRI acres not in Lawrence FCF | `10-K_20260226` |
| Lawrence stance gate | 7-year distribution DCF | `valuation.json` scenarios.base |

---

## Risks & inversion

**Primary risk:** Oil, natural gas, and NGL price declines reduce revenues and monthly distributions (`10-K` risk factors).

- **Leverage:** **$441.5 million** outstanding on a **$625 million** borrowing-base revolver at Dec 31, 2025; repayments reduce cash available for distribution (`10-K`).
- **Preferred equity:** Series cumulative convertible preferred units (Apollo-affiliated) rank senior in distributions (`10-K`, organization chart).
- **Dilution:** Equity offerings and unit issuance for acquisitions can grow unit count faster than per-unit cash (`10-K`, 424B5 shelf filings).
- **Q1 2026 softness:** Revenues **$65.5M** vs **$84.2M** prior-year quarter; net income **$0.04** vs **$0.20** per unit (`10-Q_20260507`).
- **Concentration:** Permian and Mid-Continent represent ~54% of aggregate acreage (`10-K`).

**Lens failure mode (asset):** If the market is right that distributions are peak-cycle and undeveloped acres stay undeveloped, total return equals yield minus price erosion with limited option payoff.
